# Morning Digest

**Wake up to a personalized daily learning digest, generated by AI.**

Every morning, a Claude Code agent searches the web for high-quality content matched to your interests, then commits a clean markdown digest to your private repo. You read it with your coffee. You leave quick feedback. The next day's digest gets better.

**30 minutes of learning + 15 minutes of news, zero effort to curate.**

See what a digest looks like: [`daily/sample-digest.md`](daily/sample-digest.md)

---

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) (with scheduled agents)
- [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com) (`gh`), logged in
- Git

### Setup (2 minutes)

**Step 1** — Clone this template and run the setup script:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/neelgun17/morning-digest.git
cd morning-digest
./setup.sh
```

The script creates a **private** repo, copies the templates, and pushes everything to GitHub. Your interests and digests stay private.

**Step 2** — Edit your interest profile:

```bash
cd ../my-morning-digest   # or whatever you named it
```

Open `interests.md` and replace the examples with your own interests. The file has comments explaining each field. The more specific you are, the better your digests will be.

**Step 3** — Schedule the daily agent:

Open Claude Code inside your private repo and paste this:

```
Create a scheduled trigger called "morning-digest" that runs daily at 7am my time.
Repo: <your private repo URL>
Tools needed: Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, WebSearch

Use this prompt for the agent:

You are a daily learning digest generator.

1. Read interests.md for the user's interest profile and priorities.
2. Read feedback-log.md for recent feedback.
3. Check the 3 most recent files in daily/ to avoid repeating topics. Rotate interests across the week.
4. Use WebSearch to find 2-3 high-quality articles from reputable sources matching the interests. Prioritize written content. Total read time ~30 minutes.
5. Use WebSearch to find 3-5 current news items across the user's news categories. Summarize each in 1-2 sentences with links.
6. Write the digest to daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md using today's date.
7. Include a feedback table at the bottom for inline reactions.
8. Commit and push with message "Add daily digest for YYYY-MM-DD".

Important: every link must be real and from a reputable source. Rotate across ALL interest areas throughout the week. Content should be accessible but substantive. Total: ~30 min learning + ~10-15 min news.
```

Claude Code will set up the schedule for you. Adjust the time to whenever you want your digest ready.

**That's it.** Tomorrow morning, pull your digest:

```bash
git pull
```

Or just read it on GitHub.

---

## How the Feedback Loop Works

Every digest ends with a feedback section:

```markdown
| Item | Reaction | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| Article on X | +1 | Want to go deeper on this |
| Podcast on Y | skip | Not my thing |

Want more of: practical coding tutorials
Want less of: high-level overviews
New interest to explore: woodworking
```

Just type a few words. The agent reads this before generating the next digest. You can also update `interests.md` anytime — change priorities, add new topics, remove old ones.

Over time, the system learns what you like.

---

## File Structure

```
my-morning-digest/
  interests.md        ← Your interest profile (edit this)
  feedback-log.md     ← Running feedback history
  daily/
    2026-03-26.md     ← Auto-generated daily digests
    2026-03-27.md
    ...
```

---

## Updating Your Interests

Review `interests.md` every few months. You can:

- Change **priority** (high/medium/low) to shift what appears more often
- Change **depth** (beginner/intermediate/advanced) as you learn
- Add or remove topics anytime
- Adjust your **preferences** (switch from articles to podcasts, etc.)

---

## FAQ

**Can I use a different AI model?**
Yes. When setting up the schedule, specify a different model (e.g., `claude-opus-4-6` for deeper analysis, or `claude-haiku-4-5` for faster/cheaper runs).

**Can I change the schedule?**
Yes. Run `/schedule` in Claude Code to list and update your triggers, or manage them at [claude.ai/code/scheduled](https://claude.ai/code/scheduled).

**Can I get digests on specific days only?**
Yes. Use a cron expression like `0 12 * * 1-5` for weekdays only, or `0 12 * * 1,3,5` for Mon/Wed/Fri.

**What if I want podcasts or videos instead of articles?**
Update the **Preferences** section in `interests.md`. The agent adapts to your preferred format.

**How do I stop it?**
Disable the trigger at [claude.ai/code/scheduled](https://claude.ai/code/scheduled), or run `/schedule` in Claude Code.
